home.subtitle: Understand the five phases of a cyberattack.

mascot_script.text: Hi! I'm Neko, your guide through the Cyber Kill Chain, nyaa~!
I'll walk you through seven short lessons, then a short quiz. Hit Begin Learning whenever you're ready!

lessons.label: The Framework
lessons.title: Every cyberattack tells the same story.

mascot_script.text: Welcome to lesson 1! The Cyber Kill Chain was developed by Lockheed Martin as a map of how every cyberattack moves forward.
Every breach, big or small, typically to complete each of these 5 phases to succeed.
Click any circle below to preview that phase.

intro_bullets: Developed by Lockheed Martin (2011) as a defensive framework.
Describes 5 sequential phases every attack must complete.
Breaking any single phase stops the entire attack.

chain_nodes.label: Recon
chain_nodes.name: Reconnaissance
chain_nodes.desc: The attacker researches the target, scanning networks, harvesting emails, and mapping out the organization's attack surface.

chain_nodes.label: Arm & Deliver
chain_nodes.name: Arm & Deliver
chain_nodes.desc: The attacker crafts a tailored weapon (malware or a malicious document) and delivers it via phishing or compromised channels.

chain_nodes.label: Exploit
chain_nodes.name: Exploitation
chain_nodes.desc: The weapon activates: a vulnerability is triggered, code executes, and the attacker gains their initial foothold.

chain_nodes.label: Control
chain_nodes.name: Establish Control
chain_nodes.desc: The attacker installs persistent backdoors and establishes an encrypted command channel for ongoing remote access.

chain_nodes.label: Exfiltrate
chain_nodes.name: Exfiltration
chain_nodes.desc: The endgame: sensitive data is located, compressed, and secretly transferred to the attacker's external servers.

mascot_script.text: Phase 1 · Reconnaissance. The attacker has picked ACME Corp as their target.
This is PASSIVE recon — just Google, LinkedIn, job posts, public leaks. The victim never notices a thing.
Hit Start Scan to watch them harvest employee emails from LinkedIn!
Phase 2 · Arm & Deliver. With the target list in hand, they build a weapon and pick a delivery ride.
The #1 ride? Email. Walk through the three steps to craft a malicious doc and phish poor Mike.
Phase 3 · Exploitation. Mike opens what LOOKS like an invoice. He sees a warning. Just one careless click…
Victim POV: Click the glowing Enable Content button yourself to see exactly what fires behind the scenes.
Phase 4 · Establish Control. Code is running on Mike's machine. The attacker plants backdoors and calls home.
Run each step in order to watch persistence get installed, then see the encrypted C2 tunnel come alive.
Phase 5 · Exfiltration. Mission-objective time — steal the crown jewels. This is the loud part.
Click files one at a time, or smash-and-grab everything at once. Either way, the damage is done.
Real-world time! This is MGM Resorts, September 2023. Scattered Spider used every phase you just learned, in exact order.
A single 10-minute phone call to the IT help desk caused over $100M in losses. Social engineering is scary powerful!
When you're ready, hit Start the Quiz to prove what you learned!

steps.title: LinkedIn & Social Profiling
steps.desc: Attackers profiled an MGM IT staffer by scraping LinkedIn and other social media — his role, his colleagues, even his speaking style.

steps.title: The 10-Minute Phone Call
steps.desc: They called the IT help desk impersonating the employee and convinced the agent to reset his credentials — no malware needed.

steps.title: Identity Provider Takeover
steps.desc: With the reset credential, they logged into MGM's Okta tenant and escalated privileges across the identity platform.

steps.title: Lateral Movement & Persistence
steps.desc: They moved from cloud identity into on-prem Active Directory, installing a secondary identity provider for persistent access.

steps.title: Ransomware & $100M in Losses
steps.desc: BlackCat ransomware was deployed across thousands of ESXi hosts; slot machines, room keys, and reservations went dark for 10 days.

challenges.label: Challenge 1
challenges.title: Reconstruct the Kill Chain
challenges.desc: Drag the phases into the correct order, from first to last.

mascot_script.text: First challenge! I'll shuffle the five phases — you drag them back into the correct attack order.
Remember: passive research comes first, stolen data leaves last. You got this!

challenges.label: Challenge 2
challenges.title: Classify the Attack Events
challenges.desc: Each event belongs to a phase of the kill chain. Drag each event to the correct phase.

mascot_script.text: Nice work! Now let's match real events to their phase. Drag each event card onto the phase it belongs to.
If you drop one on the wrong phase, just drag it back out and try again. Take your time!

events.text: Searched LinkedIn for employee emails and job titles
Created a malicious document and emailed it to the target
User opened the document, triggering hidden code execution
Backdoor installed and encrypted channel established to attacker
Confidential data compressed and transferred to external server

challenges.label: Challenge 3
challenges.title: Defend the Network
challenges.desc: An attack is unfolding in real-time. At each phase, choose the correct defensive action to stop the attacker before they reach their objective.

mascot_script.text: Final challenge! You're on the blue team now — an attack is unfolding and you have to pick the right defense at each phase.
Hit Start Simulation when you're ready. Five phases, one shot each — no pressure, nyaa~!

rounds.alert: An unknown entity is scanning your public infrastructure and scraping employee data from social media profiles.

options.text: Limit public exposure & deploy social engineering awareness training
Patch all known CVEs on internal application servers
Deploy DLP tools to monitor outbound data transfers

rounds.alert: A wave of spoofed emails carrying suspicious attachments is flooding employee inboxes, disguised as urgent invoices from a trusted vendor.

options.text: Monitor internal network for unusual lateral movement
Deploy email gateway filtering & sandbox suspicious attachments
Rotate all employee account passwords immediately

rounds.alert: An employee opened a document that is exploiting an unpatched vulnerability to execute malicious code on their workstation.

options.text: Patch known vulnerabilities & enforce macro-disabled policies

rounds.alert: A compromised workstation is making regular encrypted connections to an unknown overseas IP every 30 seconds.

options.text: Isolate the endpoint & block C2 traffic at the firewall
Conduct a vulnerability scan on public-facing web apps

rounds.alert: Anomalous data flows detected: 2.4 GB of compressed files being transferred to an external IP address during off-hours.

options.text: Enable multi-factor authentication across all accounts
Implement DLP monitoring & block unauthorized bulk transfers
Deploy an updated email security gateway
